---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
##library(readxl)
##library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[.center[
Use case studies to define, operationalize and measure the concept of terrorism
]]

<br>

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

### Prep for Class
1. Review submitted cases
    - https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wKZRrm5JaZAAWbkC14iGfbs6syAMHnyZCEucqvco8Rg/edit?usp=sharing

<br>

Today we kick off the third section of our class.

What explains the variation in how and why non-state actors use violence against states and people?

- Specifically, we'll focus on terrorism.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size60[**For Today**]

<br>

.size50[
Bring to class one specific piece of evidence you argue represents an act of terrorism. 

Make sure you are familiar with the actors and environment involved.
]

???

### Everybody ready for our work today?

### All citations in APA format?

<br>

Today I want us to use the case studies to build our intuitions about terrorism and to help us construct a definition and operationalization of the concept.

### Remind me, what is a concept?
- (The name we give an idea / thing)

<br>

### And what does it mean to operationalize a concept?
- (Operationalization is "...selecting observable phenomena to represent abstract concepts" (89).)
    - Brians, Craig Leonard, Lars Willnat, Jarol B. Manheim, and Richard C. Rich. 2011. “From Abstract to Concrete: Operationalization and Measurement.” In Empirical Political Analysis, Boston, MA: Longman, (ONLY p88-110)





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.center[**Terrorism Case Studies**]]

<br>

.size50[
1. What is the specific act you are calling "terrorism"?

2. Why is it "terrorism"?
]

???

I'd like each of you to introduce your case as an answer to these two questions.

1. What is the specific act you are calling "terrorism"?
    - As you describe your case, focus particularly on the actor and the immediate act.
    - Support and funding matters but let's start by agreeing and exploring this micro-level dynamic.

2. Why is it "terrorism"?

### Everybody take two minutes to make sure you're ready to present your case in this way.

<br>

Ok, let's do this!

Everybody take notes!
- As you listen you should jot down any notes you'll need to define terrorism based on our cases.

*Go around the room to share evidence*



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size60[.center[**Defining Terrorism**]]

<br>

.size60[
Make a list of the common characteristics of our cases
]

???

ON YOUR OWN: Take two minutes to reflect on all the cases in order to make a list of their common characteristics.

<br>

PAIRS: 5 minutes to share, consolidate and add to your lists

<br>

CLASS: Let's build a big list of possible characteristics

*ON BOARD*
- ?

<br>

*2019 class*
- one-sided violence
- Important purpose more important than survival
- Cause fear
- Political
- Non-state actor
- Intend to maximize casualties
- Strategic
- Claimed by or affiliated with known terror group
- Motivated to target a specific group (religion/race/ethnicity)


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size60[.center[**Defining Terrorism**]]

<br>

.size60[
Make a list of the sub-groups in our cases
]

???

Clearly our list of cases represent a "group" we call "terrorism."

- The question is, within these cases are their important groupings we should take note of?

- Think of the grocery store as the big group but within it there is a dairy section, meat section, produce section, etc.

### Make sense?

<br>

ON YOUR OWN: Take a few minutes to brainstorm groupings in our cases.
- Any obvious groupings here?
- Any cases here you think we should exclude (e.g. not sufficiently "terrorism")
- Most similar cases that are different from the rest?

<br>

PAIRS: 5 minutes to share, consolidate and add to your lists

<br>

CLASS: Let's build a big list of possible characteristics

*ON BOARD*
- ?

<br>

*2019 class*
- Domestic vs international
- Specific goal vs spur of the moment
- Unaffiliated vs affiliated with group
- Violence vs threat


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size60[.center[**Defining Terrorism**]]

<br>

.size60[
Make a list of key questions
]

???

ON YOUR OWN: Take two minutes to brainstorm key questions.
- What questions must any good definition of terrorism address?
- In other words, if two people are arguing about terrorism what would you demand they agree on to make sure their argument is productive?

<br>

PAIRS: 5 minutes to share, consolidate and add to your lists

<br>

CLASS: Let's build a big list of questions our definitions need to answer

*ON BOARD*
- ?

<br>

*2019 class*
- Fear as the goal?
- Action done by state, non-state or both?
- Targets politically motivated or other aims included (e.g. profit?)
- Proximity to the violent act (do the bombing vs send someone to do it vs funding groups who act in ways you like?)

*2015 class*
- Civilians or not?
- attacking people not related to the policy you object to
- Terror / fear important
- Connections to other terror actions
- Attacks on freedom of expression (e.g. specific policy)
- Ideological objectives too
- In response to the actions of a government actor
- Attack on a building, no deaths, symbolic violence
- Acts meant to intimidate, may or may not be related to a specific policy change
- Revenge against government overreach
- Direct opposition to a government
- Providing support to a violent group as terrorism
- Groups vs individuals
- To make a statement
- To deliver harm to a government
- Done by a "terror group"
- Targeting a government vs targeting military
- nature of the group, non-state
- Targeting government, act of protest
- Kidnapping as form of violence, targeting civilians, spreading fear 



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/10-1-terrorism_word_cloud.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

???

I hope everybody has all of this written down!

You'll need it on Wednesday as we take all of this material in order to develop a definition and operationalization of our key concept: Terrorism.
